title: Henckelia mishmiensis
taxonomy: Henckelia mishmiensis is classified as a species within the genus Henckelia, which belongs to the family Gesneriaceae, an order of flowering plants commonly known as the 'African violet family'. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf morphology and flower structure. The genus Henckelia is a group of tropical plants native to Southeast Asia. Henckelia mishmiensis specifically is native to the Mishmi Hills region in northeastern India. The classification of Henckelia mishmiensis helps in understanding its evolutionary history and its relationships with other plant species.

intro: Henckelia mishmiensis, also known as Mishmi hill gentian, is a medicinal plant species native to the Eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Henckelia mishmiensis include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional medicine, Henckelia mishmiensis is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
